stem: What single-term Pauli label does `SparsePauliOp.from_sparse_list([('Z', [0], 1.0)], num_qubits=3)` produce?
code:
from qiskit.quantum_info import SparsePauliOp

op = SparsePauliOp.from_sparse_list([("Z", [0], 1.0)], num_qubits=3)
print(op.paulis[0])

explanation.correct: `from_sparse_list` places the operator 'Z' on qubit index 0 of a 3-qubit register. Labels are little-endian: qubit 0 is the right-most character. So the Z lands on the right-most position, giving 'IIZ'.

distractors.A: 'ZII' would put the Z on the left-most character, i.e. qubit 2 (big-endian reading), not qubit 0.
distractors.B: 'IZI' places the Z on qubit 1 (the middle character), off by one from index 0.
distractors.C: 'ZZZ' applies Z to all three qubits; the sparse entry specifies only qubit index 0.
